message:
bd-3ty3s.2/.3/.4: Conformal frame guard, degradation cascade, and integration tests

Add ConformalFrameGuard (p99 prediction intervals with EMA tracking,
nonconformity scores, and JSONL evidence) and DegradationCascade
(conformal guard → budget controller → widget priority orchestration
with recovery streak tracking).

14 integration tests covering stable regime, spike detection,
progressive degradation, recovery, conformal coverage guarantee
(>95% over 1000 samples), and e2e cascade with widget filtering.

Also fix pre-existing issues: interactive.rs patch() call sites,
retry.rs dead_code warning, and markdown_rich_text.rs missing
WrapMode::Optimal arm.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3bxhj.10.37","title":"task(opentui-import alien tests): E2E graveyard-executable gauntlet (route->rank->contract->verify->demo->release)","description":"Background:
Unit validation is necessary but insufficient. We need a realistic E2E gauntlet proving that the full graveyard-executable chain behaves safely and deterministically across success and failure campaigns.

Goal:
Ship a mandatory route->rank->contract->verify->demo->release gauntlet with strong red-path diagnostics and replayability.

Gauntlet scenario families:
1) Green-path promotion:
- Valid symptom routing, accepted composition sets, complete metadata, passing verify gates, executable demos.
2) Metadata and contract faults:
- Missing headers, stale references, malformed contract clauses, absent legal/paper artifacts.
3) Composition risk failures:
- Unsafe primitive combinations, missing countermeasures, policy-threshold violations.
4) Demo/execution failures:
- demo.yaml mismatch, claim-linkage breaks, non-reproducible outputs.
5) Optimization policy failures:
- Unsafe optimization lever combinations, rollback-clause violations, non-compliant release policy interactions.

Deliverables:
- CI-ready E2E scripts with deterministic fixtures for each scenario family.
- JSONL evidence bundle with stage-level lineage and replay handles.
- Triage helper output mapping failure signature -> likely root cause cluster.

Acceptance criteria:
1) Pipeline emits deterministic stage artifacts and supports one-command replay for each failing scenario.
2) Red paths fail safely with explicit machine-checkable clauses (never implicit/opaque failure reasons).
3) Logs always include: stage_id, route_id, ranking_hash, contract_id, verify_verdict, demo_id, release_policy_verdict, replay_cmd.
4) Successful completion is a hard prerequisite for .10.45 and .9.9 release-candidate gates.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:36:08.534934156Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T23:52:12.165734441Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-graveyard","e2e","opentui-import","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.37","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:36:40.350024249Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.37","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:36:08.534934156Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.37","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:37:43.090640132Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.37","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:37:43.545178087Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.37","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T23:50:21.801271665Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.37","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.16","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T23:52:12.165676874Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.37","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.27","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:37:10.998241736Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.37","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.28","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:36:39.901894685Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.37","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.32","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:36:39.022601970Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.37","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.33","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:37:09.242984468Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.37","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.34","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:36:39.457768719Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.37","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:37:09.685354507Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.37","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.36","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:36:38.582650020Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.37","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.5.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:37:42.640672456Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3bxhj.10.38","title":"task(opentui-import alien): Implement hierarchical conjugate Bayesian evidence fusion with dependence-aware corrections","description":"Background:
OpenTUI→FrankenTUI migration evidence streams are heterogeneous, sparse in long tails, and often correlated (e.g., widget-coverage signal correlates with runtime-event signal). Naive score fusion overstates certainty and creates brittle decisions.

Goal:
Build an alien-artifact-grade evidence-fusion core that is closed-form, deterministic, and auditable under correlated weak signals.

Work packages:
1) Conjugate online kernels:
- Beta-Binomial for Bernoulli outcomes (pass/fail checks, parity flags).
- Gamma-Poisson for rate processes (event/error intensity).
- Dirichlet-Multinomial for categorical strategy outcomes.
2) Hierarchical shrinkage:
- Partial pooling across project family, component class, and widget archetype.
- Empirical-Bayes prior initialization from corpus strata to reduce variance in sparse regimes.
3) Dependence-aware fusion:
- Correlation/copula-inspired correction term to deflate double-counted evidence.
- Posterior covariance proxy exported with each fused decision.
4) Robustification:
- Huberized likelihood fallback for outlier-heavy telemetry.
- Prior-stress hooks (epsilon-contamination / prior-perturbation probes) to quantify model-risk sensitivity.
5) Diagnostics + audit artifacts:
- Posterior predictive checks and simulation-based calibration (SBC) harness entry-points.
- Deterministic evidence hash + ledger rows for replay and explainability cards.

Deliverables:
- Runtime fusion module integrated with existing evidence ledger contracts.
- JSONL artifact schema additions: posterior_state_id, shrinkage_profile_id, dependence_factor, robust_mode, sensitivity_band.
- Operator-facing diagnostics for mismatch and overconfidence detection.

Acceptance criteria:
1) All posterior updates are replay-stable and numerically stable in log-domain at extreme values.
2) Correlated channels never increase confidence without explicit dependence adjustment trace.
3) Sensitivity bands are emitted for each high-impact decision; high sensitivity auto-raises conservative action policy.
4) Predictive-check failures trigger explicit degraded-confidence flags consumed by downstream policy gates.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:39:02.741504654Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:02.512214829Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact-coding","bayesian","opentui-import"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.38","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:39:02.741504654Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.38","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.21","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:01.635158562Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.38","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.23","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:02.073744935Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.38","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.29","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:02.512136953Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3bxhj.10.39","title":"task(opentui-import alien): Build sequential multiple-testing control with e-BH + alpha-investing wealth management","description":"Background:
The migration pipeline evaluates many hypotheses over time (quality regressions, parity claims, release-worthiness checks). Classical fixed-horizon p-value logic breaks under optional stopping and asynchronous gate order.

Goal:
Implement anytime-valid, multi-hypothesis control that preserves statistical validity while remaining operationally practical.

Work packages:
1) Sequential evidence core:
- e-value constructors for each gate family (quality, performance, accessibility, determinism).
- Safe composition rules for sequential and batched updates.
2) Multiple-testing control:
- Primary controller: e-BH over concurrent gate families.
- Secondary policy profiles: alpha-investing wealth process for online arrival of tests.
- Optional advanced profile hooks for LORD/SAFFRON-style online FDR where appropriate.
3) Wealth governance:
- Per-family wealth ledgers with transfer/clamp policies.
- Starvation prevention and fairness budgeting between high- and low-volume gate families.
4) Policy integration:
- Hard fallback rules when wealth is exhausted or evidence is adversarially noisy.
- Deterministic replay from event stream with no order ambiguity.
5) Observability:
- Structured artifacts for e_value, rejection_threshold, wealth_before, wealth_after, family_id, and decision clause.

Deliverables:
- Runtime sequential-FDR controller package with policy config schema.
- Audit trail and explainability surfaces for wealth trajectory and rejection rationales.
- Gate adapter for release policy consumption.

Acceptance criteria:
1) Type-I/FDR control remains valid under optional stopping and arbitrary asynchronous execution order.
2) Wealth process is deterministic and replay-identical for fixed seed/input logs.
3) Budget depletion always triggers explicitly logged conservative mode (never silent permissive behavior).
4) Controller emits machine-checkable clauses linking each reject/retain decision to e-value and wealth state.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:39:03.177459819Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:03.836500424Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact-coding","e-process","opentui-import"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.39","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:39:03.177459819Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.39","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.17","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:03.393384970Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.39","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.23","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:02.950000322Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.39","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.30","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:03.836459598Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-3bxhj.10.40","title":"task(opentui-import alien): Implement counterfactual decision-audit solver and minimal-flip explanations","description":"Background:
Users and operators need decision transparency that is not hand-wavy: for each migration verdict, we must be able to answer what minimally would have needed to change for a different action to be optimal.

Goal:
Provide formal counterfactual decision audits with minimal-flip explanations that are reproducible and policy-aware.

Work packages:
1) Counterfactual problem formalization:
- Encode decision rule as constrained optimization over evidence terms and loss matrix parameters.
- Support L1/L2/weighted perturbation norms and policy constraints (immutable evidence, bounded edits).
2) Solver layer:
- Deterministic minimal-perturbation search (MILP/SAT-compatible abstraction where needed).
- Tie-break policy for multiple equivalent perturbation sets.
3) Action-sensitivity analysis:
- Compute nearest alternative action and perturbation distance.
- Emit feature-level and group-level perturbation attributions.
4) Governance integration:
- Attach counterfactual artifact IDs to decision claim records.
- Provide blocking rule when decision is excessively fragile (small perturbation norm below configured threshold).
5) UX + audit exposure:
- JSON/ledger output + human-readable summary for explainability cards and review workflows.

Deliverables:
- Counterfactual engine with deterministic solver settings.
- Artifact schema: decision_id, baseline_action, alt_action, perturbation_set, perturbation_norm, satisfiable, fragility_band.
- Policy hook to require mitigation when fragility is high.

Acceptance criteria:
1) Every promoted decision has at least one satisfiable minimal-flip counterfactual or an explicit unsat proof artifact.
2) Re-running the solver on identical input yields identical perturbation set and norm.
3) Fragile decisions trigger explicit policy clauses and cannot bypass release gates silently.
4) Counterfactual artifacts are linked to claim/policy IDs and replay commands.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:39:03.605553882Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:05.170678145Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact-coding","explainability","opentui-import"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.40","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:39:03.605553882Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.40","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:04.725658525Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.40","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.22","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:04.287830502Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.40","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.33","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:05.170629704Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3bxhj.10.41","title":"task(opentui-import alien): Build robust missing-evidence graceful-degradation policy compiler","description":"Background:
Real migration runs regularly face partial telemetry, delayed traces, contradictory probes, and MNAR-style missingness. We need graceful degradation that preserves safety and debuggability instead of brittle failure.

Goal:
Compile explicit degradation policy contracts so decisions remain safe, interpretable, and recoverable under incomplete evidence.

Work packages:
1) Degradation DSL:
- Encode missingness classes (MCAR/MAR/MNAR proxies), contradiction classes, and evidence-quality tiers.
- Specify action clamps, uncertainty inflation rules, and max-risk envelopes per tier.
2) Robust inference fallback:
- Deterministic imputation families with provenance tags (carry-forward, conservative priors, bounded worst-case).
- Confidence inflation and loss penalization under degraded evidence.
3) State machine:
- Entry/exit/cooldown semantics for degraded mode.
- Hysteresis thresholds to prevent oscillation between normal/degraded states.
4) Adversarial resilience:
- Contradictory-evidence arbitration rules and anomaly-triggered clamp mode.
- Escalation hooks for manual operator review in ambiguous red zones.
5) Recovery proofs:
- Criteria for re-entering full-confidence mode tied to measurable evidence-quality improvements.

Deliverables:
- Degradation policy compiler + runtime evaluator.
- Artifact schema: degradation_reason, evidence_quality_score, inflation_factor, clamp_action, recovery_guard.
- Deterministic replay traces for degraded-path decisions.

Acceptance criteria:
1) No decision path fails catastrophically when any configured evidence subset is missing/delayed/contradictory.
2) Every degraded decision emits explicit reason codes, uncertainty deltas, and clamp clauses.
3) Recovery to normal mode requires satisfied measurable guards; no implicit auto-promotion.
4) Degraded paths remain deterministic and auditable end-to-end.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:39:04.047412733Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:06.542960525Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact-coding","graceful-degradation","opentui-import"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.41","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:39:04.047412733Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.41","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.22","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:06.083002159Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.41","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:06.542910421Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.41","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.38","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:05.620397116Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3bxhj.10.42","title":"task(opentui-import alien): Implement guarantee-assumption registry and automatic applicability checker","description":"Background:
Formal guarantees are only trustworthy when their assumptions are explicit and continuously checked (exchangeability, stationarity windows, bounded drift, contamination limits, independence approximations).

Goal:
Create a first-class assumption/guarantee contract layer that prevents invalid guarantee claims from entering release decisions.

Work packages:
1) Assumption registry:
- Typed assumption catalog with IDs, formal definitions, observables, and violation thresholds.
- Mapping from each guarantee mechanism (conformal/e-process/PAC-Bayes/sequential-FDR) to required assumption sets.
2) Applicability checker:
- Runtime and gate-time evaluators computing assumption satisfaction scores from telemetry/evidence logs.
- Contradiction/falsification detectors for unstable regimes.
3) Guarantee downgrade policy:
- Automatic downgrade/invalidation rules when assumptions are violated.
- Conservative fallback action mapping for each downgraded guarantee class.
4) Proof artifacts:
- Emit per-decision guarantee certificate including assumption hash, applicability verdict, and downgrade rationale.
- Cross-link guarantee certificate with decision claim + release gate records.
5) Operator diagnostics:
- Human-readable plus machine-checkable reports for why a guarantee is valid/invalid.

Deliverables:
- Assumption registry schema + checker engine.
- Artifact schema additions: guarantee_id, assumption_set_hash, applicability_score, violated_assumptions, fallback_clause.
- Integration contract for release policy and explainability surfaces.

Acceptance criteria:
1) Every guarantee claim in the pipeline references explicit assumption IDs and a computed applicability verdict.
2) Assumption violations block unsafe promotion paths and force conservative fallback with logged rationale.
3) Guarantee certificates are reproducible and replay-identical for identical inputs.
4) No guarantee can be surfaced as 'valid' without passing checker thresholds.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:39:04.495423705Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:07.911743952Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-artifact-coding","formal-guarantees","opentui-import"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.42","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:39:04.495423705Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.42","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.23","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:06.998532868Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.42","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.31","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:07.458366861Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.42","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.10.35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:45:07.911702164Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-3bxhj.6","title":"epic(opentui-import): Validation, certification, and confidence scoring","description":"## Background
Provide certification-grade proof that translated FrankenTUI outputs preserve semantics and improve quality where allowed by contract.

## Scope
- Baseline harnesses, trace capture/replay, and differential comparators (semantic/visual/perf/accessibility).
- Determinism soak, fuzz/adversarial scenarios, and verdict policy engine.
- Machine-checkable proof artifacts for semantic-preservation obligations.
- Unit + E2E certification pipelines with full evidence ledgers and replay diagnostics.

## Success Criteria
1. Certification verdicts are reproducible and traceable to comparator evidence and contract clauses.
2. Proof artifacts and witness integrity checks pass for required preservation obligations.
3. Determinism soak/fuzz pipelines surface divergences with minimized replayable counterexamples.
4. CI-ready E2E gates block release when certification thresholds are not met.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:28:21.390109139Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T22:22:56.668811746Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["certification","opentui-import","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:28:21.390109139Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-3ty3s.1","title":"task(e2e): E2E test suite for Recipe B Tail-Safe Adaptive","description":"## E2E Integration Test: Recipe B Tail-Safe Adaptive Controller (EV=recipe)

### Scope
Full integration test inducing drift, verifying fallback triggers, and logging conformal/e-process/BOCPD convergence. Validates p99 stays bounded under all conditions.

### Test Scenario
1. Initialize FrankenTUI with Recipe B controller active (expected-loss + conformal + e-process + BOCPD).
2. Phase 1 - Steady state (frames 0-100): stable 60fps, verify adaptive behavior.
3. Phase 2 - Gradual drift (frames 100-200): slowly increase frame times from 8ms to 18ms.
4. Verify BOCPD detects changepoint within 20 frames of drift onset.
5. Verify e-process crossing triggers sequential test alarm.
6. Verify conformal prediction intervals widen to accommodate drift.
7. Phase 3 - Fallback (frames 200-250): verify safe-mode activates (deterministic conservative policy: full diff, no coalescing, no degradation).
8. Phase 4 - Recovery (frames 250-350): drift reverses, verify controller re-adapts.
9. Throughout all phases: verify p99 frame time stays < 20ms (tail-safe guarantee).

### JSONL Structured Logging Specification
Each frame emits:
```json
{
  "event": "recipe_b_frame",
  "ts_ns": u64,
  "frame_id": u64,
  "phase": "steady|drift|fallback|recovery",
  "conformal_interval": { "lower": f64, "upper": f64 },
  "conformal_coverage": f64,
  "e_process_value": f64,
  "e_process_crossed": bool,
  "bocpd_run_length": u32,
  "bocpd_changepoint_prob": f64,
  "expected_loss_action": "string",
  "safe_mode_active": bool,
  "frame_time_ms": f64,
  "p99_frame_time_ms": f64,
  "p99_bounded": bool
}
```

### Success Criteria
- BOCPD detects drift within 20 frames of onset (changepoint_prob > 0.8).
- E-process crosses threshold during drift phase.
- Safe mode activates before p99 exceeds 20ms.
- p99_bounded == true for ALL frames (the tail-safe guarantee).
- Recovery phase re-enables adaptive behavior within 50 frames.
- No panics, no unsafe code (#![forbid(unsafe_code)]).
- JSONL log is schema-compliant and parseable.
- Conformal coverage maintains >= 95% throughout test.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:26:15.812899903Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:25.749785273Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","logging","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ty3s.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3ty3s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:26:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3ty3s.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3ty3s.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:26:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-3u5x","title":"Text Effects: Animation Timing Utilities","description":"## Background
Multiple text effects need time-based animation but there's no unified timing system. This causes:
- Inconsistent speed units across effects
- No way to pause/resume animations globally
- No delta-time handling for frame-rate independence

## Implementation

Add AnimationClock struct:
```rust
pub struct AnimationClock {
    time: f64,           // Current time in seconds
    speed: f64,          // Time multiplier (1.0 = normal, 0.0 = paused)
    last_tick: Instant,  // For delta calculation
}

impl AnimationClock {
    pub fn new() -> Self { ... }
    
    pub fn tick(&mut self) {
        let now = Instant::now();
        let delta = now.duration_since(self.last_tick).as_secs_f64();
        self.time += delta * self.speed;
        self.last_tick = now;
    }
    
    pub fn time(&self) -> f64 { self.time }
    pub fn set_speed(&mut self, speed: f64) { self.speed = speed; }
    pub fn pause(&mut self) { self.speed = 0.0; }
    pub fn resume(&mut self) { self.speed = 1.0; }
    pub fn reset(&mut self) { self.time = 0.0; }
}
```

### Speed Unit Convention:
All effects use **cycles per second**:
- speed: 1.0 = one full cycle per second
- speed: 0.5 = one cycle every 2 seconds
- speed: 2.0 = two cycles per second

Document this in all effect beads.

### Integration with StyledText:
```rust
impl StyledText {
    pub fn time(mut self, time: f64) -> Self {
        self.time = time;
        self
    }
    
    // Or use a clock reference
    pub fn with_clock(mut self, clock: &AnimationClock) -> Self {
        self.time = clock.time();
        self
    }
}
```

## Files
crates/ftui-extras/src/text_effects.rs

## Unit Tests
- test_clock_advances: time() increases after tick()
- test_clock_pause_stops: time() frozen when paused
- test_clock_speed_multiplies: speed=2 doubles time advancement
- test_clock_reset_zeros: reset() sets time to 0

## Integration
This bead should be implemented FIRST or in parallel with Easing, as many other effects depend on consistent timing.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: Animation Timing Utilities
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
